description: Cloud-based CRM platform for managing sales, service, marketing, and customer data
auth: OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST, SOAP, GraphQL, Bulk, Streaming, and Metadata APIs covering nearly all CRM objects and platform features

description: Cloud-based CRM and marketing/sales/service platform for managing contacts, deals, marketing, and customer support
auth: OAuth2 (public apps) and private-app access tokens (API key-like bearer tokens)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST APIs (v3) covering CRM objects, marketing, sales, service, CMS, workflows, webhooks, plus GraphQL for CRM data

description: Sales-focused CRM platform for managing pipelines, deals, contacts, and sales activities
auth: API key (token) and OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API v1 and v2 covering deals, persons, organizations, activities, products, leads, pipelines, webhooks, etc.

description: Attio is a flexible, data-model-driven CRM platform for managing companies, people, deals, and sales workflows.
auth: API key (bearer access token) and OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform REST API covering objects/records, lists, notes, tasks, comments, webhooks, and workspace members; docs at developers.attio.com

description: Open-source CRM platform for managing contacts, companies, deals/opportunities, and custom objects.
auth: API key (Bearer token); OAuth2 also supported for some integrations
api_surface: Extensive, documented REST and GraphQL API covering full CRUD on people, companies, opportunities, tasks, notes, and custom objects, plus webhooks; broad enough to cover most core CRM data operations

description: Customizable work-management and CRM platform where teams build apps/workflows to track projects, contacts, and deals
auth: OAuth2 (with API key/Client ID + Client Secret registration)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API covering apps, items, tasks, spaces, contacts, files, comments, hooks, and more (100+ endpoints)

description: Cloud-based CRM software for managing sales pipelines, leads, contacts, and customer engagement across channels.
auth: OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented REST API (v2/v6) covering modules, records, notes, workflows, functions, bulk read/write, and metadata across the whole CRM object model

description: Close is an inside-sales CRM combining pipeline management with built-in calling, email, and SMS communication tools.
auth: API key (HTTP Basic Auth, key as username/blank password); OAuth2 also supported for published integrations
api_surface: extensive, full-platform REST API (leads, contacts, opportunities, tasks, calls, SMS, emails, custom fields, search, webhooks, reporting) with interactive docs/explorer at developer.close.com

description: Copper is a CRM built for Google Workspace that helps sales teams manage contacts, companies, leads, opportunities, and pipelines.
auth: API key (token + user email header) and OAuth2 (Authorization Code Grant, non-expiring tokens)
api_surface: extensive - documented REST API (developer.copper.com) covering people, companies, leads, opportunities, projects, tasks, activities, custom fields, and webhooks; multiple third-party MCP servers already exist (e.g. 39-tool and 32-operation implementations)

description: DealCloud is a deal, relationship, and pipeline management CRM platform built for private equity, investment banking, and other capital markets firms.
auth: OAuth2 (client credentials flow using client_id + API key to obtain a bearer token)
gated_reason: API keys/credentials can only be generated by an existing DealCloud customer admin from within a live tenant (via user Profile settings), so access requires a paid DealCloud subscription rather than free/trial developer signup
api_surface: documented REST API covering data (objects/entries), schema, lists, users, files, and reporting endpoints plus Python/C# SDKs — moderately broad, full platform CRUD but tenant-scoped rather than a large public multi-tenant API
blocker: requires an existing paid DealCloud tenant with admin-enabled API key/OAuth credentials; no public free-tier or sandbox for outside developers

description: Cloud-based customer service and support ticketing platform with helpdesk, live chat, and knowledge base tools
auth: OAuth2 (primary/recommended) and API token with Basic auth (legacy, deprecated Apr 2027)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST APIs covering Tickets, Users, Organizations, Help Center, Chat, Talk, Search, Webhooks, etc. (hundreds of endpoints)

description: Customer messaging platform providing live chat, helpdesk, and AI-powered support (Fin) for businesses to communicate with customers
auth: OAuth2 (and API key/access tokens for private apps)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented REST API (api.intercom.io) covering contacts, conversations, tickets, articles, admins, companies, data attributes, plus webhooks and a Canvas Kit for building apps; no official GraphQL API

description: Cloud-based customer support helpdesk software for ticketing, omnichannel support, and knowledge base management.
auth: API key (HTTP Basic Auth, key as username, any string as password)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST v2 API covering tickets, contacts, companies, agents, groups, conversations, solutions/knowledge base, time entries, and more (dozens of endpoints)

description: Front is a customer communication/helpdesk platform that unifies email, chat, and messaging channels into shared team inboxes with automation and collaboration tools.
auth: OAuth2 and API key (API tokens)
api_surface: extensive, full platform REST API (Core API covering conversations, messages, contacts, inboxes, tags, teammates, channels, rules, etc.) plus a webhooks and application/OAuth framework

description: B2B customer support/helpdesk platform (shared Slack/Teams support, ticketing, knowledge base) for companies to manage customer issues and accounts
auth: API key (Bearer token)
api_surface: Documented REST API covering issues, accounts, contacts, teams, users, knowledge base, and 'me' endpoints — moderate breadth, roughly 20-30 endpoints; official remote MCP server also exists at mcp.usepylon.com (OAuth-based)

description: LiveAgent is a cloud-based helpdesk and live chat platform that unifies email, chat, calls, and social media into one ticketing system.
auth: API key
api_surface: Extensive, full-platform REST API v3 (tickets, contacts, companies, tags, departments, agents, chat, etc.), documented per-instance at https://YOURDOMAIN.ladesk.com/docs/api/v3/; official Python client also exists

description: Plain is a modern B2B customer support / helpdesk platform (shared inbox, ticketing, Slack-based support) built for software companies
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive, full-platform GraphQL API covering threads, customers, tenants, labels, workflows, webhooks, etc.

description: Help Scout is a customer support/helpdesk platform providing shared inbox, live chat, knowledge base, and customer messaging tools.
auth: OAuth2 (authorization code and client credentials grants); legacy API key support also documented
api_surface: extensive, full platform - Mailbox API v2 (REST/JSON) covers conversations, customers, mailboxes, users, tags, workflows, webhooks, and more, plus separate Docs API for knowledge base

description: Gorgias is an ecommerce-focused helpdesk platform that centralizes customer support tickets, live chat, and social/email channels with automation and AI agents.
auth: API key (Basic Auth) for private apps; OAuth2 for public/marketplace apps
api_surface: extensive, full platform REST API (tickets, customers, tags, macros, integrations, satisfaction surveys, teams, etc.) plus an official MCP server at mcp.gorgias.com/mcp (beta, included on every helpdesk plan)

description: Gladly is a customer service platform that unifies all channels (voice, email, chat, SMS, social) into a single customer-centric timeline for agents.
auth: token (API token via HTTP Basic Auth in Authorization header)
gated_reason: API tokens can only be created by a user with the 'API User' permission inside an existing paid Gladly account/admin console — no public self-signup or free trial for API access.
api_surface: moderate, REST API (developer.gladly.com/rest) covering customers, conversations, tasks, payments, and app platform/webhooks — not a huge endpoint count but reasonably full-featured for helpdesk workflows
blocker: Requires an existing paid Gladly customer account with admin-level API User permission to generate credentials; no open developer signup

description: Team messaging and collaboration platform with channels, DMs, and workflow automation.
auth: OAuth2 (plus bot/user tokens and legacy API tokens for internal apps)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - Web API with 200+ REST-style methods (chat, conversations, users, files, admin, workflows) plus Events API, Socket Mode, and Block Kit; some admin/Enterprise Grid scopes require workspace admin approval

description: Cloud communications platform providing programmable APIs for SMS, voice, WhatsApp, video, and email messaging.
auth: API key (Account SID + Auth Token / API Key-Secret, Basic Auth over HTTPS), with OAuth2 available for some Twilio products
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented REST API with 1,800+ endpoints across 30+ products (SMS, Voice, Video, WhatsApp, Verify, etc.)

description: Business team chat/messaging platform with channels, bots, and workflow automation.
auth: OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API v2/v3 covering messages, channels, chats, bots, users, plus a bot/webhook platform (incoming/outgoing webhooks, message handlers, slash commands, widgets)

description: All-in-one collaboration suite (chat/messaging, docs, sheets, calendar, approval, drive) with an extensive Open Platform API for building custom apps and bots
auth: OAuth2 (user_access_token) plus app-credential tokens (App ID/App Secret -> tenant_access_token/app_access_token)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - hundreds of REST endpoints across messaging, bots/events, docs, sheets, calendar, contacts, approval, drive, attendance

description: Pumble is a team chat and messaging app (Slack alternative) offering channels, DMs, and video calls for workplace communication.
auth: API key
api_surface: narrow, documented REST API (~10-20 endpoints) covering messaging, channels, and users, aimed at bots/integrations
blocker: API surface is limited mainly to messaging/bot use cases, lacking deeper admin/workspace management endpoints, and no existing MCP server

description: Discord is a chat platform providing voice, video, and text communication organized into servers and channels for communities and teams.
auth: OAuth2 and bot token (API key-style)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented REST API plus real-time Gateway (WebSocket) covering messaging, channels, guilds/servers, users, voice, webhooks, interactions/slash commands, and more (hundreds of endpoints/events)

description: Cloud-based instant messaging platform offering chat, voice/video, channels, and bots via its Bot API and client (MTProto) API.
auth: token (bot token via BotFather for Bot API; api_id/api_hash + phone auth for MTProto client API)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - Bot API has 100+ methods (messages, media, inline queries, payments, webhooks, groups/channels management) plus a separate low-level MTProto Client API for full user-account automation

description: Cloud API letting businesses send/receive WhatsApp messages, manage templates, phone numbers, and business profiles programmatically.
auth: OAuth2 / token (Meta system-user and business-integration access tokens, Bearer auth)
gated_reason: Free test-number sandbox exists, but production use requires a verified Meta Business Manager account, phone number registration, and Meta approval of message templates/display name.
api_surface: extensive, full platform - Graph API-based endpoints for messages, media, templates, phone numbers, business management, webhooks, flows, and commerce/catalog
blocker: Business verification and template/number approval gate production access even though the API itself is well-documented and broad

description: Cloud-based phone system and call center software with click-to-dial, call routing, and CRM/helpdesk integrations
auth: Basic (api_id/api_token) for own-account apps; OAuth2 for marketplace/partner apps
api_surface: extensive, full platform - calls, contacts, users, teams, numbers, webhooks, SMS, IVR/dialer endpoints documented at developer.aircall.io

description: Cloud communications platform providing APIs for SMS, voice calls, WhatsApp/RCS messaging, verification/2FA, and video.
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive, full platform (SMS, Voice, Verify, WhatsApp/RCS Messages, Video, Number Insight, JWT-secured Applications API) with signup and free trial credit

description: API for programmatically creating, managing, and reporting on Google Ads advertising campaigns.
auth: OAuth2 (plus a required Google-issued developer token)
gated_reason: Requires a Google Ads developer token whose production-level access must be manually reviewed and approved by Google; only limited test-account access is available immediately.
api_surface: extensive, full platform (Google Ads API covers campaigns, ad groups, ads, bidding, reporting via GAQL, conversions, audiences, etc. across hundreds of resources); no official MCP server, though some community/unofficial MCP wrappers exist
blocker: Production API access requires Google's developer token approval process (basic/standard access review), which can take days to weeks and requires an active Google Ads account and compliance with Google's ads policies.

description: Meta's Marketing API lets developers create, manage, and analyze ad campaigns across Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and Audience Network programmatically.
auth: OAuth2
gated_reason: Requires a Meta developer app, an ad account, and Meta App Review/Business Verification (advanced access) to use most Marketing API permissions in production.
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST-based Graph API covering campaigns, ad sets, ads, creatives, audiences, insights/reporting, and more (100+ endpoints/fields)
blocker: App Review and Business Verification gating (advanced access, ad account permissions) add friction beyond simple self-serve API key/OAuth signup

description: REST APIs for creating and managing LinkedIn ad campaigns, creatives, audience targeting, and performance reporting.
auth: OAuth2
gated_reason: Requires a verified Company Page, an approved developer app, and LinkedIn's manual review/vetting to be granted the Advertising API product before any credentials work.
api_surface: extensive - full platform of REST APIs (Campaign Management, Creatives, Ad Analytics/Reporting, DMP Segments/audience targeting, Conversions API, Company Intelligence), covering most ad account lifecycle operations
blocker: API access requires LinkedIn's manual app review and Advertising API product approval (often 1-4 weeks, rejections common), gating self-serve/free access despite a broad, well-documented API surface.

description: All-in-one CRM/marketing platform (by HighLevel) for managing contacts, funnels, calendars, campaigns, and communications for agencies and their clients.
auth: OAuth2 (Authorization Code Grant with refresh tokens); also supports Private Integration Tokens for direct/location-level access
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API v2 covering Contacts, Conversations, Opportunities, Calendars, Payments, Workflows, Locations, Custom Fields, etc. (public docs + GitHub repo)

description: Email marketing and marketing automation platform for creating campaigns, audiences, and reports
auth: API key, OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform (Marketing API covers lists/audiences, campaigns, automations, templates, reports, e-commerce, plus Transactional/Mandrill API separately)

description: Email/SMS marketing automation and customer data platform for e-commerce and D2C brands
auth: API key (private key) and OAuth2 for server-side; public key for client-side
api_surface: extensive, full platform REST API covering profiles, campaigns, flows, lists, segments, events, metrics, templates, catalogs, and more (100+ endpoints)

description: All-in-one marketing platform for building sales funnels, email campaigns, online courses, and affiliate programs.
auth: API key
api_surface: Documented public REST API covering contacts, tags, courses/enrollments, webhooks, and more (moderate breadth, ~30-40 endpoints); some resources like communities and course/student management are still on the public roadmap; no MCP server exists yet.
blocker: API coverage is still incomplete for some resources (e.g. communities, deeper course/student management are requested but not yet available), limiting full platform automation.

description: Lets developers manage Pinterest boards, pins, ads campaigns, and analytics programmatically for business accounts.
auth: OAuth2
gated_reason: Apps must submit a request form and pass Pinterest's manual review (trial access) before receiving API credentials; higher 'standard'/advanced access tiers require additional review and business verification.
api_surface: extensive - REST API v5 (v5.3.0) covering pins, boards, user accounts, ads/campaigns, catalogs, and analytics; roughly 50+ endpoints
blocker: No official Pinterest MCP server; only unofficial community-built ones exist, and API access requires manual trial-app approval plus tiered access escalation for full ads/analytics scopes.

description: Lets developers programmatically publish posts (text/image/video/carousel), manage replies, and retrieve insights/profile data for the Threads social network.
auth: OAuth2
gated_reason: A Meta developer app can be created for free, but publishing/reading on behalf of any user beyond your own test accounts requires Meta App Review and Business Verification before production access is granted.
api_surface: narrow-to-moderate, ~15-20 REST (Graph API-style) endpoints covering publishing, replies, insights, and profile management; no GraphQL
blocker: Production use requires Meta App Review approval and business verification, limiting immediate self-serve agent access to developer's own test account

description: Cloud-based email delivery platform for sending transactional and marketing emails at scale via API or SMTP
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive, full platform - v3 REST API covers mail send, templates, marketing campaigns/contacts, stats, suppressions, webhooks, subusers, IP pools, and more (100+ endpoints)

description: Ecommerce platform letting merchants build, run, and scale online stores, with APIs to manage products, orders, customers, and storefronts.
auth: OAuth2 (also token exchange / access tokens for custom apps)
api_surface: extensive, full platform — GraphQL Admin API (primary, thousands of fields/types across products, orders, customers, inventory, fulfillment, etc.), REST Admin API (legacy, being phased down), Storefront API, and Partner/CLI APIs; official Storefront MCP, Customer Accounts MCP, and Shopify Dev MCP servers already exist

description: Open-source WordPress ecommerce plugin whose REST API lets developers manage store data like products, orders, customers, and coupons programmatically
auth: API key (Consumer Key/Secret via OAuth1.0a query auth over HTTP, or Basic Auth over HTTPS)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API covers products, variations, orders, refunds, customers, coupons, reports, taxes, shipping, webhooks, and settings (dozens of endpoints); official docs at woocommerce.github.io/woocommerce-rest-api-docs

description: Cloud ecommerce platform providing REST/GraphQL APIs to manage products, orders, customers, carts, and storefronts
auth: OAuth2 (app auth) and API key (store-level API accounts with tokens)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - hundreds of REST endpoints (Catalog, Orders, Customers, Carts/Checkout, Webhooks) plus a GraphQL Storefront API; no official MCP server found yet

description: Enterprise cloud e-commerce platform (B2C/B2B Commerce Cloud) for building and managing online storefronts, merchandising, checkout, and order management.
auth: OAuth2 (JWT bearer access tokens via Client Credentials or Account Manager; SCAPI/OCAPI API clients)
gated_reason: Requires an existing Commerce Cloud org/license and Business Manager admin access to register an API client in Salesforce Account Manager; no free public signup for API credentials
api_surface: extensive, full platform - SCAPI (B2C Commerce API) offers Shopper, Admin, and Data APIs (products, baskets, orders, customers, pricing, promotions, etc.) plus legacy OCAPI (deprecated April 2026); dozens of resource endpoints across Shopper/Admin APIs
blocker: Requires a provisioned Commerce Cloud sandbox/instance and admin-created API client credentials (no self-serve trial credentials), limiting out-of-the-box agent access

description: Adobe's enterprise ecommerce platform (Magento Open Source / Adobe Commerce) for building and managing online storefronts, catalogs, orders, and checkout.
auth: OAuth2 (OAuth 1.0a for third-party integrations, Adobe IMS for SaaS/Cloud), token-based Bearer auth, and API keys for admin/customer sessions
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented REST API (hundreds of endpoints across catalog, orders, customers, inventory, CMS) plus a full GraphQL API for storefront queries/mutations; community MCP servers already exist (e.g. magento-mcp, adobe-commerce-dev-mcp, Freento MCP module) and Adobe announced an official MCP server at Summit 2026

description: Squarespace's Commerce APIs let developers manage products, inventory, orders, transactions, profiles, and webhooks for Squarespace-hosted online stores.
auth: OAuth2 (for registered Extensions) and API keys (Bearer token) for merchant-generated access
gated_reason: API keys can only be generated by a site owner on a paid Squarespace Commerce plan, and OAuth client registration requires submitting an app for Squarespace review/approval as a registered Extension
api_surface: Documented REST API covering Products, Inventory, Orders, Transactions, Profiles, and Webhook Subscriptions (~6 resource areas, moderate breadth, not a full platform API); no official MCP server, though several third-party/community MCP servers exist
blocker: Requires a paid Commerce plan and store setup to get API keys, or a reviewed/approved OAuth Extension registration, limiting free/self-serve access

description: Ecwid is an ecommerce platform/storefront builder that lets merchants add online stores to websites, marketplaces, and social channels.
auth: OAuth2 (plus long-lived private/public access tokens for custom apps)
gated_reason: API access requires the store to be on a paid Ecwid plan (or a free plan upgrade requested from Ecwid for a dev/integration project) before tokens/scopes can be issued
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API covering products, categories, orders, customers, discounts, storefront, profile, and app market/OAuth endpoints (dozens of resources)
blocker: Requires a paid store plan (or manual free-upgrade request) to unlock API access, adding a gating step beyond pure self-serve signup

description: Gumroad lets creators sell digital products, memberships, and services directly to consumers, with an API to manage products, sales, subscribers, and licenses
auth: OAuth2
api_surface: documented REST API (v2) covering products, variants, offer codes, sales, subscribers, resource subscriptions, and license verification — narrow-to-moderate, roughly 30-40 endpoints across ~8 resource types; no official or widely-known MCP server exists

description: Suite of REST APIs (SP-API) letting Amazon sellers and vendors programmatically manage orders, listings, inventory, pricing, reports, and fulfillment.
auth: OAuth2
gated_reason: Requires a registered Amazon Professional Seller/Vendor account and app registration; public apps need Amazon review/approval (especially for restricted PII data access roles), private apps require self-authorization via Seller/Vendor Central.
api_surface: extensive, full platform - 30+ API sections (Orders, Listings, Catalog, Reports, Feeds, FBA Inventory, Pricing, Finances, Notifications, etc.) covering hundreds of endpoints
blocker: Access requires an active paid Amazon seller/vendor account plus Amazon's app approval/authorization workflow (OAuth via Login with Amazon), which limits pure self-serve trial access for a generic agent toolkit.

description: Payments/checkout platform (rebranded Commas) for creators and merchants, offering checkout pages, subscriptions, funnels, BNPL financing, and payment orchestration.
auth: API key
api_surface: Documented REST API (apidocs.fan) covering checkout sessions, customers, subscriptions, discount codes, and webhooks, plus SDKs for Node/Python/PHP/Ruby and a Postman collection — moderate breadth, ~20-30 endpoints, not a full extensive platform

description: Provides pay-as-you-go REST APIs for SERP scraping, keyword research, backlinks, on-page SEO, and other search/marketing data.
auth: Basic (API login/password via HTTP Basic Auth header)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - dozens of endpoints across SERP, Keywords Data, Backlinks, On-Page, DataForSEO Labs, Content Analysis, Business Data, Domain Analytics modules

description: SEO platform providing keyword research, rank tracking, backlink analysis, site audits, and SERP/AI-visibility data via REST APIs
auth: API key (token, via Authorization header or token param)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - Data API (keyword research, backlinks, domain performance, SERP, site audits, AI visibility) plus Project API (rank tracking, keyword grouping, competitor/AI visibility monitoring) across six documented endpoint groups, all JSON REST

description: SEO toolset (Site Explorer, Keywords Explorer, Rank Tracker, Site Audit) with a REST API v3 exposing backlink, keyword, and site-audit data.
auth: API key (Bearer token), with OAuth2 also available for Ahrefs Connect integrations
gated_reason: API v3 access requires an active Lite-tier or higher paid subscription (Starter and free plans excluded), and keys are managed only by workspace owners/admins
api_surface: extensive, full platform — covers Site Explorer (backlinks, organic search, referring domains), Keywords Explorer, Rank Tracker, Site Audit, and account/subscription endpoints; consumption metered via API units
blocker: No free/trial tier for API access — requires a paid Lite+ subscription and consumes metered API units, limiting frictionless agent onboarding

description: AI-assisted no-code web scraper builder that lets users create scrapers via natural language/point-and-click and run them at scale with rotating proxies.
auth: API key (Bearer token)
api_surface: narrow, single main endpoint (scrapers-ai-rerun) to trigger/rerun scrapers created in the UI plus token management; not a broad general-purpose REST platform
blocker: API only reruns scrapers pre-built in the MrScraper UI (no way to define/configure a scraper purely via API), so an agent would still depend on a human-configured scraper before it can call the endpoint

description: Cloud platform for building, running, and sharing web scrapers and automation tools ('Actors') via API, with a store of thousands of ready-made scrapers.
auth: API key (Bearer token, also supports OAuth for MCP)
api_surface: extensive, full platform REST API (Actors, datasets, key-value stores, webhooks, schedules, users, storage) plus official JS/Python API clients

description: API that turns any website into clean, LLM-ready data via scraping, crawling, mapping, and structured extraction
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive: REST API covering scrape, crawl, map, search, batch scrape, and structured extract endpoints, plus SDKs

description: Web data platform providing proxies, web unlocker, scraping APIs, and pre-built datasets for extracting public web data at scale
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST APIs covering Web Scraper/Crawl API, Web Unlocker, Dataset Marketplace, Browser API, SERP API, plus a CLI; official MCP server already exists

description: Open-source Python CLI tool that hunts down social media accounts by username across hundreds of social networks
auth: other
api_surface: No official hosted API; it is a local CLI/Python tool run against site-specific URLs, and a community-built experimental Django REST wrapper (sherlock-project/api) exists but has been unmaintained since 2021
blocker: No official maintained API or auth model to build against — only a CLI you'd have to wrap yourself, or an abandoned experimental REST project

description: B2B data enrichment API that aggregates 30+ vendors to return verified emails, phone numbers, and company/contact intelligence in real time.
auth: API key
api_surface: narrow, ~5-6 REST endpoints (Prospector, Contact Enrichment, Phone Enrichment, Company Enrichment, Email Verification, job-change detection) with async/webhook support; no GraphQL

description: Clay is a GTM data enrichment and workflow automation platform that lets users build tables to scrape, enrich, and route contact/company data using 100+ integrated data providers and AI.
auth: API key (Bearer token), with support for Basic Auth and JWT-based flows for custom HTTP API integrations
gated_reason: Core HTTP API/webhook integrations require a paid Growth plan (~$495/mo), and the full 'Enterprise API' for people/company data is only available on Enterprise contracts (~$30K+/yr); the free tier does not include API/webhook access
api_surface: Narrow: primarily inbound webhooks to trigger/populate Clay table workflows and an HTTP API step for calling external APIs from within tables; a separate, more limited Enterprise API exists just for people/company data lookups (not a general table CRUD API). No broad public REST/GraphQL platform API.
blocker: No full public REST/GraphQL API for managing tables/workflows programmatically—access is webhook/trigger-based and gated behind paid Growth or Enterprise plans; existing MCP server is an unofficial third-party (webhook-wrapping) project, not an official Clay-maintained API

description: Hosts Git repositories and provides collaboration tools (issues, pull requests, actions, packages) for software development
auth: OAuth2 and token (personal access tokens / GitHub App installation tokens)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API (docs.github.com/rest) covers repos, issues, PRs, actions, packages, orgs, users, webhooks, etc., plus a parallel GraphQL API; thousands of endpoints/fields

description: Cloud platform for deploying and hosting frontend/web applications, providing programmatic control over deployments, projects, domains, and infra.
auth: token (Bearer personal access tokens / API keys), OAuth2 for integrations and the official MCP server
api_surface: extensive, full platform — 40+ resource categories (deployments, projects, domains, DNS, teams, env vars, edge config, webhooks, firewall, artifacts, logs, etc.) covering nearly all dashboard functionality

description: Netlify is a platform for building, deploying, and hosting web applications and static/serverless sites with CI/CD, edge functions, and CDN.
auth: OAuth2 (for public integrations) and personal access token (Bearer token) for direct API/CLI use
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented REST API (open-api.netlify.com) covering sites, deploys, forms, DNS, functions, env vars, teams, and more; well beyond ~10 endpoints

description: Cloudflare provides a global edge network platform (CDN, DNS, security, Workers serverless compute, R2 storage, and more) accessible via a comprehensive REST API.
auth: API key / token (API tokens with scoped permissions; legacy Global API Key; OAuth used for the hosted MCP server)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API (developers.cloudflare.com/api) covering 2,500+ endpoints across DNS, Workers, R2, KV, D1, Zero Trust, Analytics (GraphQL), and virtually every Cloudflare product

description: Open-source Firebase alternative providing a hosted Postgres database, auth, storage, edge functions, and realtime APIs for building backends.
auth: API key (project anon/service keys) plus OAuth2/personal access tokens for the Management API
api_surface: extensive, full platform: auto-generated REST (PostgREST) and GraphQL APIs per project, plus a separate Management REST API covering ~all org/project admin operations (projects, database, auth, storage, functions, secrets)

description: Neo4j is a native graph database platform that stores, queries, and analyzes highly connected data using the Cypher query language.
auth: Basic (username/password) and Bearer token
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented HTTP/Query API (Cypher over HTTP, transactions, discovery endpoints), Bolt driver protocol, plus separate Aura management REST API; broader than a narrow single-resource API

description: Cloud data platform for data warehousing, engineering, sharing, and AI/ML workloads (Cortex) with a SQL/REST API surface.
auth: OAuth2, token (key-pair JWT / Personal Access Token)
api_surface: Extensive, full platform: SQL API for query execution plus REST APIs for account/object management (databases, warehouses, users, tasks), Cortex AI (Analyst, Search, Agents) endpoints, and Snowpark; free trial account gives full API access.

description: MongoDB Atlas is a fully managed cloud database platform (DBaaS) offering an Administration API to programmatically provision and manage clusters, users, backups, and networking.
auth: API key (HTTP Digest auth, public/private key pair) with OAuth2 via Service Accounts also supported
api_surface: extensive, full platform - the Atlas Administration API v2 (REST) covers hundreds of endpoints spanning clusters, projects, users, backups, network access, alerts, billing, search, and more; official SDKs exist for Go, Java, Python, etc.

description: Cloud monitoring and observability platform for infrastructure metrics, logs, APM traces, and security signals
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive, full platform - hundreds of REST endpoints across metrics, logs, APM, monitors, dashboards, incidents, security, and more (docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/)

description: Application monitoring platform for error tracking, performance monitoring, and debugging of software applications.
auth: API key (personal/organization auth tokens) and OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API with hundreds of endpoints covering projects, issues, events, releases, teams, alerts, and more

description: All-in-one workspace API for managing pages, databases, blocks, comments, and users in Notion
auth: OAuth2 and API key (internal integration token)
api_surface: documented REST API, moderate breadth (~20-30 endpoints covering pages, databases, blocks, users, search, comments); official hosted MCP server exists at mcp.notion.com

description: Cloud-based spreadsheet-database hybrid for building custom apps, workflows, and relational data tables
auth: OAuth2, token (Personal Access Token via Bearer header)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API covering bases, tables, records, fields, views, webhooks, attachments, and metadata (legacy API keys deprecated Feb 2024 in favor of PATs/OAuth)

description: Linear is an issue tracking and project management tool for software teams, offering a full GraphQL API for managing issues, projects, cycles, and workflows.
auth: OAuth2 (PKCE, recommended for third-party apps) and personal API key (Bearer token) for account-level access
api_surface: extensive, full platform GraphQL API covering issues, projects, cycles, comments, labels, milestones, initiatives, documents, attachments, webhooks, and more

description: Jira is Atlassian's issue-tracking and project management tool for planning, tracking, and managing agile software development and business workflows
auth: OAuth2 (3-legged OAuth via Atlassian Identity, OAuth 2.0 3LO for Connect/Forge apps), also supports API tokens with Basic auth for simple integrations
api_surface: extensive, full platform - Jira Cloud REST API v3/v2 covers issues, projects, workflows, boards, sprints (Jira Software), users, permissions, webhooks, and JQL search; hundreds of endpoints documented at developer.atlassian.com

description: Cloud-based work and project management platform for tracking tasks, projects, and team workflows
auth: OAuth2, token (Personal Access Token)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - RESTful API covering workspaces, projects, tasks, users, tags, custom fields, portfolios, goals, webhooks, etc.

description: Cloud-based Work OS for managing projects, workflows, and team collaboration via customizable boards.
auth: OAuth2 (for apps/integrations) and personal API tokens (token-based)
api_surface: extensive, full platform — single GraphQL endpoint (api.monday.com/v2) covering boards, items, columns, users, workspaces, webhooks, and apps/automations

description: ClickUp is a cloud-based productivity platform for task/project management, docs, goals, and team collaboration.
auth: API key (personal token) and OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API v2 covering tasks, lists, folders, spaces, docs, goals, time tracking, webhooks, etc.; MCP server already exists

description: Coda is an all-in-one doc/spreadsheet/app platform combining documents, tables, and automations for team workflows.
auth: API key (Bearer token)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API (v1) covering docs, pages, tables, rows, columns, formulas, controls, permissions, folders, publishing, automations, analytics, plus separate Admin API

description: Cloud-based work management and automation platform for tracking projects, workflows, and collaborative spreadsheets/sheets.
auth: OAuth2 (also supports personal access/API tokens)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - documented REST API covering sheets, rows, columns, cells, attachments, workspaces, reports, dashboards, webhooks, users, and admin resources

description: Time tracking, invoicing, and expense management software for businesses and freelancers
auth: OAuth2 and Personal Access Token (token)
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API covering time entries, projects, tasks, clients, invoices, estimates, expenses, users, and reports

description: Payment processing platform providing APIs for online payments, billing, subscriptions, and financial infrastructure.
auth: API key (restricted/secret keys), with OAuth2 also supported for Connect platform integrations and the remote MCP server
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API covering payments, customers, subscriptions, invoices, refunds, disputes, Connect, Billing, and more (hundreds of endpoints)

description: Plaid provides APIs that let apps securely connect to users' bank accounts to retrieve balances, transactions, identity, income, and other financial data.
auth: API key (client_id + secret headers), with OAuth2 used in the Plaid Link bank-login flow
gated_reason: Sandbox/Development credentials are self-serve via the Dashboard, but live Production access to real bank data requires Plaid's approval/compliance review and a pricing agreement
api_surface: extensive, full platform - dozens of REST endpoints across products (Auth, Identity, Balance, Transactions, Assets, Liabilities, Investments, Income, Transfer, etc.)
blocker: Production (real financial data) access is gated behind Plaid's approval/compliance process, not fully self-serve for agent-tooling use

description: Binance's API platform lets developers programmatically access spot, margin, futures, and other crypto trading services, market data, and account/wallet management.
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST, WebSocket, and FIX APIs covering spot/margin/futures trading, market data, wallet, staking, sub-accounts, and more across dozens of docs pages with hundreds of endpoints

description: A white-label payment gateway (built on NMI's infrastructure) for processing card payments, tokenized vaulting, and recurring billing.
auth: API key
gated_reason: API security keys are generated in the merchant control panel, but you must first have an approved NMI/reseller merchant account (typically via an ISO/payment processor partnership), so there's no free/anonymous developer signup.
api_surface: extensive, full platform - Direct Post/Query APIs, v4 API, Customer Vault (tokenization), recurring billing, and Gateway.js/Payment Component for tokenized checkout
blocker: Requires a provisioned merchant account and merchant-portal-issued security key before any API access is possible; not something a developer can self-provision without a merchant/reseller relationship

description: Forensic FX audit platform that scores international payment transactions against live mid-market rates to surface hidden bank/card FX fees and markups.
auth: API key (Bearer token)
api_surface: narrow, ~4-10 endpoints (check_fx_rate, audit_transaction, full_forensic_report, compare_fx_sources plus REST API/SDKs) covering FX audit and reporting; enterprise/white-label tier is contact-sales gated

description: QuickBooks Online is Intuit's cloud accounting platform for invoicing, bookkeeping, payments, and financial reporting for small businesses
auth: OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API (Accounting API plus Payments, Payroll add-ons) covering Invoices, Customers, Payments, Bills, Vendors, Reports, and more, with free sandbox companies and webhooks for real-time sync

description: Cloud accounting software for small businesses covering invoicing, bank reconciliation, payroll, and financial reporting.
auth: OAuth2
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST APIs across Accounting, Payroll, Files, Assets, Projects, Payments, and Finance, each with dozens of endpoints

description: Corporate card, spend management, and expense platform providing APIs for transactions, cards, expenses, budgets, and payments
auth: OAuth2 (authorization code / client credentials) and static API key (bearer token)
gated_reason: API tokens require an Account Admin or Card Admin role on an existing paid Brex account, and OAuth partner integrations require Brex approval of redirect URIs/client credentials
api_surface: extensive, full platform - REST API covering transactions, cards, expenses, budgets, users, payments, accounting, and onboarding, plus scopes system; an official first-party MCP server also exists

description: Ramp is a corporate card and spend management platform providing APIs for transactions, cards, reimbursements, accounting, bills, and users.
auth: OAuth2 (client credentials and authorization code grants)
gated_reason: Requires an existing Ramp business account, and only Admin or Business Owner roles can create a developer app and grant OAuth2 scopes (sandbox access is available but tied to a Ramp customer account).
api_surface: extensive - full platform covering transactions, cards, reimbursements, bills, accounting, users, limits, and more, documented as versioned REST API (v1)
blocker: Admin approval and an active Ramp business account are needed to provision OAuth2 credentials, limiting self-serve access for independent developers

description: Provides private/public market financial data (companies, deals, investors, funds, LPs, valuations) via a research platform and data API.
auth: API key
gated_reason: API requires a separate paid contract, account-manager approval, and a requested sandbox key — no public self-signup.
api_surface: documented REST API (PB-Token auth, api.pitchbook.com) with relational endpoints across deals, companies, investors, funds, people, LPs, service providers — moderately broad but credit-metered and contract-gated; no MCP server found
blocker: Requires existing PitchBook client status plus a separate paid API contract and account-manager-issued sandbox key before any integration work can start.

description: Enterprise version of Google's AI notebook/research assistant that lets users upload documents and data sources to generate summaries, Q&A, and audio overviews grounded in their own content.
auth: OAuth2 (Google Cloud IAM / service account credentials)
gated_reason: Requires a paid NotebookLM Enterprise or Gemini Enterprise license tied to a Google Cloud project with billing/IAM setup, not a free self-serve API key.
api_surface: narrow, ~10-15 endpoints (notebooks.create, sources management, batch upload) via google.cloud.notebooklm.v1alpha REST/gRPC API, plus Gemini Enterprise integration endpoints
blocker: Requires paid Google Cloud enterprise licensing and IAM-based project setup before any API access is possible; no free tier or public MCP server exists yet

description: AI meeting assistant that records, transcribes, and summarizes conversations with action items and insights
auth: API key (Bearer token)
gated_reason: Public API is in beta and restricted to Enterprise plan customers who must contact their Otter account manager to enable access and generate a token
api_surface: narrow-to-moderate, documented REST API (beta) covering channels, conversations, transcripts, audio, action items, insights, outlines, and workspace details with cursor-based pagination and rate limits (~10 req/s); an official Otter MCP Server also exists for Enterprise customers
blocker: API and MCP server require an Enterprise plan plus manual enablement via account manager; no self-serve trial keys available

description: AI meeting recorder/notetaker that records, transcribes, and summarizes video calls, generating action items and CRM-ready notes.
auth: API key
api_surface: Documented REST API at api.fathom.ai/external/v1 (developers.fathom.ai) covering meetings, recordings/transcripts, summaries, action items, teams, and webhooks — narrow-to-moderate, roughly a dozen endpoints plus webhook registration; TypeScript/Python SDKs provided.

description: AI-powered search engine that finds and synthesizes answers from peer-reviewed research papers
auth: API key (REST), OAuth2 (MCP server)
gated_reason: API access is 'by application only' with per-call pricing plus a platform fee, requiring a custom quote/approval rather than free self-service signup
api_surface: narrow, single documented v1 quick_search endpoint (api.consensus.app/v1/quick_search) plus a hosted MCP server at mcp.consensus.app/mcp
blocker: Official REST API requires applying and getting a custom quote/approval (not self-serve free tier), though a hosted OAuth-based MCP server already exists

description: Agentic document intelligence platform providing APIs for parsing, splitting, extracting, editing, and classifying complex documents (PDFs, scans, tables) into LLM-ready structured data.
auth: API key
api_surface: extensive, full platform — documented REST API (public OpenAPI 3.1 spec) with Parse, Extract, Split, Edit, Classify, Pipeline, Cite endpoints plus async/job variants and an Upload API; official Python/Node SDKs

description: Devin is an autonomous AI software engineer that plans, writes, tests, and ships code, exposed via a REST API and MCP server for programmatic session/task management.
auth: API key (Bearer token, cog_ prefix; service-user keys or personal access tokens)
gated_reason: API/MCP access requires a paid Team ($80+/mo) or Enterprise plan; the free and individual Pro tiers do not include API access.
api_surface: documented REST API (v1/v2/v3, 'External API') covering sessions, playbooks, knowledge base, integrations, API keys - moderate breadth, full platform management rather than just core coding tasks; official first-party MCP server (mcp.devin.ai) already wraps it
blocker: Requires purchasing/upgrading to a paid Team or Enterprise plan to unlock API/MCP access; no free or trial path to credentials

description: AI content-generation platform/CLI for creating cinematic images, videos, 3D assets, and audio via 40+ generative models, usable by developers and AI agents.
auth: API key (key + secret pair, Bearer/env-var based)
api_surface: Documented REST API (Higgsfield Cloud API) with official Python and Node.js/TypeScript SDKs and a CLI wrapping generation endpoints (image, video, 3D, audio, analysis) across 40+ models — moderate breadth, not a huge platform but broader than a single-endpoint tool.

description: Command-line tool (mmdc) that renders Mermaid diagram definition files into SVG, PNG, or PDF using a headless Chromium/Puppeteer instance.
auth: other
api_surface: No hosted REST/GraphQL API - it's a local npm CLI/Node.js library (@mermaid-js/mermaid-cli) invoked as a subprocess or imported programmatically; input/output is via files or stdio, not network endpoints. An MCP server already exists (e.g. peng-shawn/mermaid-mcp-server) wrapping this exact rendering functionality.

description: REST API for extracting YouTube video transcripts, plus search, channel, and playlist data.
auth: API key (Bearer token)
api_surface: narrow, ~6 documented REST endpoints (transcripts, search, channel search/browse/latest, playlist extraction)

description: Grain records, transcribes, and uses AI to summarize video meetings, extracting notes, highlights, and insights for sales and product teams.
auth: OAuth2 (Authorization Code + PKCE) and API key
gated_reason: API/OAuth client registration and full API access require a paid Grain plan (Business/Enterprise tier), not available on free accounts
api_surface: documented REST API (developers.grain.com) covering recordings, highlights, insights, views, and webhooks — narrow-to-moderate, not a full platform; no MCP server found
blocker: API access gated behind paid plan tiers and OAuth app approval; no existing MCP server to build on
